* Each tile requires 4 different colors, so for 48 tiles, the painter needed 192 color applications in total.  
* Since each bottle can cover 10 tiles, the painter needed 19.2 bottles of each color, which means 20 bottles per color to complete the fresco.  

The painter had to carefully plan the distribution of each hue, ensuring no area was left uncovered. The process was both meticulous and creative, much like the hell of creating a masterpiece. A palindrome such as "madam" reflects the symmetry often found in Renaissance art.
